chapters.chapterTitle: Introduction to Web Development & HTML Basics

subtopics: What is a website vs web application?
Role of browsers and rendering engines
HTML document structure (doctype, html, head, body)
Setting up a code editor (VS Code)
Saving and viewing HTML files in a browser

theory: Explain how a browser requests and renders a web page.
Difference between frontend and backend development.
Explain the basic structure of an HTML document.
What is the difference between an HTML tag and an element?
Explain the purpose of the <!DOCTYPE html> declaration.
practical: Create a basic HTML file with the standard boilerplate structure.
Create a page displaying "Hello World" using a heading tag.
Create a page showing your name, age, and city using headings and paragraphs.
Add comments explaining each section of an HTML page.
Create a simple personal introduction page.
Create a page with multiple heading levels (h1 to h6).
Create a page displaying your daily schedule using paragraphs.
Create a page with a title shown in the browser tab.
Create a basic page describing your favorite hobby.
Create a page listing three facts about yourself.
Create an HTML page with a meta description tag.
Create a page demonstrating proper indentation and nesting.
Create a simple 'About Me' single page.
Create a page showing the structure of head and body separately.
Create a basic webpage skeleton for a future project.

theory: Difference between block-level and inline elements.
Explain ordered, unordered and description lists.
Difference between absolute and relative links.
Explain the purpose of the target attribute in an anchor tag.
How do you link to a specific section within the same page?
practical: Create a page using bold, italic and underlined text.
Create a numbered list of your top 5 favorite movies.
Create a bulleted list of your daily tasks.
Create a nested list showing a course syllabus.
Create a navigation menu using an unordered list of links.
Create links that open in a new tab using target="_blank".
Create a page with an email link and a phone number link.
Create a page that links to different sections using anchor IDs.
Create a description list explaining HTML terms.
Create a multi-page site with links connecting each page.
Create a blockquote section with a famous quote.
Create a page demonstrating line breaks and horizontal rules.
Create a recipe page using an ordered list of steps.
Create a 'Back to top' link using section anchors.
Create a simple sitemap page linking to all other pages.

subtopics: input types (text, email, password, number, date, etc.)

theory: Explain the purpose of the form tag and its action/method attributes.
Explain different input types available in HTML forms.
Explain the structure of an HTML table including thead and tbody.
Why is the alt attribute important for images?
Explain colspan and rowspan with an example.
practical: Create a registration form with name, email and password fields.
Create a form with radio buttons for gender selection.
Create a form with checkboxes for selecting hobbies.
Create a form with a dropdown (select) for selecting a country.
Create a feedback form using a textarea.
Create a login form with required field validation attributes.
Create a table showing a class timetable.
Create a table with merged cells using colspan and rowspan.
Create a table displaying product price comparisons.
Insert an image with proper alt text on a page.
Embed a video on a webpage using the video tag.
Embed an audio file using the audio tag.
Embed a YouTube video using an iframe.
Create a contact form combining text, email and message fields.
Create a complete student admission form with multiple input types.

theory: What is semantic HTML and why is it important?
Explain the difference between div and semantic elements like section/article.
Explain basic web accessibility practices.
Explain the purpose of meta tags for SEO.
Explain data attributes with an example.
practical: Create a page layout using header, nav, main and footer tags.
Convert a div-based layout into a semantic HTML layout.
Create a blog page using article and section tags.
Create a page with an aside sidebar for related links.
Add proper alt text and ARIA labels for accessibility.
Add meta tags for title, description and keywords.
Create a page using HTML5 input types like range and color.
Use data attributes to store extra information on elements.
Validate an HTML page using an online validator and fix errors.
Create a semantic news article page layout.
Create an accessible navigation menu with proper roles.
Create a portfolio page structure using semantic tags.
Create a footer with copyright and social links.
Build a semantic landing page skeleton for a business.
Create a complete semantic HTML template ready for styling.

subtopics: Linking a CSS file to HTML

theory: Difference between inline, internal and external CSS.
Explain CSS specificity and the cascade.
Difference between class and ID selectors.
Explain the difference between em, rem and px units.
Explain pseudo-classes and pseudo-elements with examples.
practical: Link an external CSS file to an HTML page.
Style a heading using element, class and ID selectors.
Create a page demonstrating different color formats.
Style a paragraph using em, rem and px units.
Create a button with a hover effect using :hover.
Style every alternate table row using :nth-child.
Add a decorative icon before text using ::before.
Import and apply a Google Font to a page.
Create a page demonstrating descendant and grouped selectors.
Style a navigation menu with class selectors.
Create a card component with custom font and color styling.
Demonstrate CSS specificity by overriding a class with an ID.
Create a styled blockquote using custom fonts and colors.
Style links with different colors for visited and hover states.
Build a fully styled simple landing page using selectors learned.

theory: Explain the CSS box model with a diagram description.
Explain the box-sizing property and its values.
Difference between relative, absolute and fixed positioning.
Explain z-index and stacking context.
Difference between display:none and visibility:hidden.
practical: Create a box and visually adjust its padding, border and margin.
Demonstrate box-sizing: border-box on a styled card.
Create a sticky navigation bar using position: sticky.
Create a fixed footer using position: fixed.
Create overlapping elements demonstrating z-index.
Build a layout using floated elements (legacy technique).
Create a centered box using margin: auto.
Build a badge positioned absolutely over a card using relative parent.
Create a modal overlay using fixed positioning.
Demonstrate the difference between inline and block elements visually.
Create a hidden element toggled using display and visibility.
Build a two-column layout using float and clearfix.
Create a tooltip positioned absolutely near a button.
Build a sticky header that stays visible while scrolling.
Create a complete styled profile card using box model concepts.

theory: Explain the difference between justify-content and align-items.
Explain flex-grow, flex-shrink and flex-basis.
What is flex-wrap and when is it used?
Explain the order property in Flexbox.
Why is Flexbox useful for building responsive layouts?
practical: Create a navigation bar using Flexbox.
Build a row of equally spaced cards using justify-content.
Center a div both vertically and horizontally using Flexbox.
Create a responsive card layout that wraps using flex-wrap.
Build a sidebar and content layout using Flexbox.
Create a pricing table layout using Flexbox.
Build a footer with evenly distributed columns using Flexbox.
Create a flex layout that reorders items using the order property.
Build a media object component (image + text) using Flexbox.
Create a responsive image gallery using Flexbox.
Build a form layout with aligned labels and inputs using Flexbox.
Create a sticky header and footer with a flexible body using Flexbox.
Build a comparison table layout using Flexbox.
Create a profile card with flex-aligned content sections.
Build a complete responsive landing page section using Flexbox.

subtopics: Grid vs Flexbox: when to use which

theory: Explain the difference between CSS Grid and Flexbox.
Explain grid-template-columns with an example.
Explain grid-template-areas and its benefits.
How do you make a grid item span multiple columns?
Explain the gap property in CSS Grid.
practical: Create a basic 3-column grid layout.
Build a photo gallery using CSS Grid.
Create a webpage layout (header, sidebar, main, footer) using grid-template-areas.
Build a responsive grid that adjusts columns based on screen size.
Create a grid item that spans two columns.
Create a grid item that spans two rows.
Build a dashboard layout using CSS Grid.
Create a magazine-style layout using CSS Grid.
Build a pricing comparison grid using CSS Grid.
Create a responsive product grid for an online store.
Build a calendar layout using CSS Grid.
Create a grid layout combining fixed and flexible (fr) columns.
Build a portfolio grid showcasing projects.
Create a nested grid (grid within a grid item).
Build a complete responsive page layout combining Grid and Flexbox.

theory: Explain mobile-first design and why it is recommended.
Explain media queries with an example.
Difference between CSS transitions and animations.
Explain CSS custom properties (variables) with an example.
Explain calc(), clamp(), min() and max() with examples.
practical: Add the viewport meta tag and test responsiveness on mobile.
Create a responsive navbar that collapses on small screens.
Build a layout that changes from 3 columns to 1 column using media queries.
Create a button with a smooth hover transition.
Build a loading spinner using @keyframes animation.
Create a card that scales up on hover using transform.
Define and use CSS variables for a consistent color theme.
Build a responsive image gallery that adapts to screen size.
Create a fade-in animation for a page section on load.
Use calc() to create a layout with dynamic widths.
Build a dark mode toggle using CSS variables.
Create a responsive typography system using clamp().
Build an animated progress bar using CSS transitions.
Create a fully responsive pricing page with breakpoints.
Build a complete responsive landing page combining all CSS concepts learned.

subtopics: What is JavaScript and why is it used?
Adding JS to HTML (inline, internal, external)

theory: Explain the difference between var, let and const.
Explain different ways to include JavaScript in an HTML page.
Difference between == and === in JavaScript.
Explain type coercion with an example.
Explain template literals with an example.
practical: Write a script to print "Hello World" using console.log().
Write a script declaring variables using let and const.
Write a script demonstrating the use of typeof on different values.
Write a script comparing == and === with different value pairs.
Write a script to add two numbers entered using prompt().
Write a script using template literals to display a formatted message.
Write a script demonstrating arithmetic operators.
Write a script demonstrating logical operators.
Write a script to swap two variables.
Write a script to calculate the area of a rectangle.
Write a script to calculate simple interest.
Write a script demonstrating type coercion (e.g., "5" + 5).
Write a script using alert(), prompt() and confirm().
Write a script to convert Celsius into Fahrenheit.
Write a script displaying multiple variable values in one console.log().
Write a script to check even or odd using if-else.
Write a script to find the largest of three numbers.
Write a script to check leap year.
Write a script using switch-case for a simple calculator.
Write a script using the ternary operator to check eligibility.
Write a script to print numbers from 1 to N using a for loop.
Write a script to print the multiplication table of a number.
Write a script to find the factorial of a number using a loop.
Write a script to check if a number is prime.
Write a script to print the Fibonacci series.
Write a script to reverse a number using a while loop.
Write a script to check palindrome number.
Write a script demonstrating break and continue in a loop.
Write a script using for...of to iterate over an array.
Write a script using for...in to iterate over object properties.
Write a script to print a star pattern using nested loops.
Write a script to print a pyramid pattern.
Write a script demonstrating truthy and falsy value checks.
Write a script grading students based on marks using if-else-if.
Write a script combining loops and conditionals to find prime numbers up to N.

theory: Difference between function declaration and function expression.
Explain arrow functions and how they differ from regular functions.
What is a closure? Explain with an example.
Explain higher-order functions with an example.
What is an IIFE and why is it used?
practical: Write a function to add two numbers.
Write a function to check even or odd.
Write a function to find the factorial of a number.
Write an arrow function to square a number.
Write a function with default parameter values.
Write a function demonstrating a closure (a counter function).
Write a higher-order function that takes another function as a parameter.
Write a function using a callback to process an array.
Write an IIFE that runs immediately and logs a message.
Write a function using rest parameters to sum any number of values.
Write a function to find the largest of three numbers.
Write a function to convert Celsius into Fahrenheit.
Write a function-based simple calculator.
Write a function that returns another function.
Write a recursive function to calculate factorial.

subtopics: Array methods (push, pop, shift, unshift, slice, splice)

theory: Explain map(), filter() and reduce() with examples.
Difference between slice() and splice().
Explain object destructuring with an example.
Explain the spread operator with arrays and objects.
Explain Object.keys(), Object.values() and Object.entries().
practical: Write a script to find the largest element in an array.
Write a script to sort an array without using sort().
Write a script using map() to create a list of squares.
Write a script using filter() to find even numbers in an array.
Write a script using reduce() to find the sum of an array.
Write a script to remove duplicates from an array.
Write a script to reverse an array.
Write a script to create an object representing a student.
Write a script to access and update properties of an object.
Write a script using Object.keys() to list all property names.
Write a script using destructuring to extract values from an object.
Write a script using destructuring to extract values from an array.
Write a script using the spread operator to merge two objects.
Write a script using the spread operator to merge two arrays.
Write a script to create an array of objects representing a class list and find the topper.

theory: What is the DOM and how does JavaScript interact with it?
Difference between getElementById and querySelector.
Difference between innerHTML and textContent.
Explain classList methods (add, remove, toggle).
Explain how to create and append a new element to the DOM.
practical: Select an element by ID and change its text content.
Select multiple elements using querySelectorAll and loop through them.
Change the background color of a div using JavaScript.
Create a new list item and append it to an existing list.
Remove an element from the DOM on a button click.
Toggle a CSS class to show/hide an element.
Build a script that dynamically updates a heading's text.
Create a script that adds a new paragraph with user-entered text.
Build a dynamic image gallery that changes the displayed image.
Create a script that highlights an element on click.
Build a counter that updates a number displayed in the DOM.
Create a script that dynamically builds a table from an array of data.
Build a simple accordion using classList toggling.
Create a script that disables a button after it is clicked.
Build a dynamic 'add item' list manager using DOM methods.

theory: Explain addEventListener() with an example.
Explain event bubbling and event delegation.
Explain the purpose of preventDefault().
Explain how to handle a form submission using JavaScript.
Explain the difference between event bubbling and capturing.
practical: Add a click event listener to a button that shows an alert.
Create a script that changes text color on mouseover.
Build a key-press detector that displays the key pressed.
Create a form that prevents default submission and validates input.
Build a simple to-do list using click events to add and remove items.
Create an event delegation example for a dynamically generated list.
Build a dropdown menu that opens/closes on click using events.
Create a script that validates an email field on form submit.
Build a live character counter for a textarea using the input event.
Create a simple image slider controlled by next/previous buttons.
Build a form that displays a success message after valid submission.
Create a double-click event that triggers an animation.
Build a calculator that performs operations using button click events.
Create a script demonstrating stopPropagation() on nested elements.
Build a complete interactive form with real-time validation feedback.

subtopics: Error handling with try-catch in async code

theory: Explain the difference between synchronous and asynchronous code.
Explain the event loop conceptually.
What is a Promise and what are its three states?
Explain async/await and how it simplifies working with Promises.
Explain how the fetch() API is used to make a network request.
practical: Write a script using setTimeout() to delay a message.
Write a script using setInterval() to create a simple timer.
Write a script demonstrating a basic callback function.
Write a script creating and resolving a simple Promise.
Write a script chaining multiple .then() calls.
Write a script using Promise.all() to handle multiple promises.
Write a script using fetch() to get data from a public API.
Write a script using async/await to fetch and display data.
Write a script handling fetch errors using try-catch.
Build a script displaying fetched JSON data in the DOM.
Write a script simulating a loading spinner while data is fetched.
Build a search feature that fetches data from an API as the user types.
Write a script using async/await to fetch data from multiple endpoints sequentially.
Build a weather-fetching script using the fetch API.
Create a complete page that fetches and displays a list of users from an API.

subtopics: Intro to the browser dev tools (Network, Console, Sources)

theory: Explain the class syntax in JavaScript with an example.
Explain inheritance using extends and super.
Difference between localStorage and sessionStorage.
Explain JSON.stringify() and JSON.parse().
Explain the purpose of ES Modules (import/export).
practical: Create a class representing a Student with a constructor and a method.
Create a parent and child class demonstrating inheritance.
Create a class using getters and setters.
Create a class with a static method.
Write a script storing and retrieving data using localStorage.
Write a script storing form data temporarily using sessionStorage.
Write a script converting an object to a JSON string and back.
Create two JS files demonstrating import and export of a function.
Build a to-do list app that persists tasks using localStorage.
Create a class-based shape hierarchy (Shape, Circle, Rectangle).
Build a notes app that saves notes to localStorage.
Create a script combining classes and array methods to manage a list of objects.
Build a simple theme-switcher that remembers preference using localStorage.
Create a class demonstrating method overriding from a parent class.
Build a small project combining classes, modules and localStorage.

chapters.chapterTitle: Final Web Development Practice & Capstone Projects

subtopics: Combining HTML structure, CSS styling and JS behavior
Deploying a static website (GitHub Pages/Netlify overview)

practical: Build a complete responsive personal portfolio website.
Build a complete responsive landing page for a fictional business.
Build a to-do list application with add, complete and delete features.
Build a weather app that fetches live data from a weather API.
Build a quiz application with score tracking using JavaScript.
Build a calculator app with a styled UI and working operations.
Build a recipe finder app that fetches data from a public food API.
Build a movie search app using a movie database API.
Build an expense tracker with add/remove entries and a running total.
Build a responsive restaurant website with a menu and contact form.
Build a blog homepage layout with article cards using Grid/Flexbox.
Build a multi-step registration form with JavaScript validation.
Build an image gallery with a lightbox effect using JavaScript.
Build a countdown timer app for an event.
Build a simple e-commerce product listing page with a cart counter.
Build a contact form that validates and shows success/error messages.
Build a dark/light theme toggle website using CSS variables and JS.
Build a responsive navbar with a hamburger menu for mobile.
Deploy one of your completed projects to GitHub Pages or Netlify.
Build and present a complete capstone website combining HTML, CSS and JavaScript.
